name: Python Scientific Compute
description: Generic CUDA-ready scientific Python template for linear algebra, simulation, and analytics workloads.

params.script:
import json, os, time, torch

size = int(os.getenv('MATRIX_SIZE', '4096'))
device = 'cuda' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu'
a = torch.randn(size, size, device=device)
b = torch.randn(size, size, device=device)
t0 = time.time()
c = a @ b
if device == 'cuda':
    torch.cuda.synchronize()
elapsed = time.time() - t0

print('DC1_RESULT_JSON:' + json.dumps({
  'template': 'python-scientific-compute',
  'device': device,
  'matrix_size': size,
  'elapsed_s': round(elapsed, 4),
  'checksum': round(float(c.mean().item()), 6),
  'status': 'completed'
}))

model_cache.behavior: Cold cache is eviction-friendly for transient artifacts generated by ad-hoc scientific jobs.

params.script:
import json, torch

device = 'cuda' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu'
a = torch.randn(512, 512, device=device)
b = torch.randn(512, 512, device=device)
c = a @ b
print('DC1_RESULT_JSON:' + json.dumps({'template':'python-scientific-compute','device':device,'matrix_size':512,'checksum':round(float(c.mean().item()),6),'status':'completed'}))
